content: DELVE introduces a bottom‑up unsupervised feature selection framework that first identifies dynamic co‑varying feature modules via local neighborhood analysis, then ranks all features by their association with a trajectory graph constructed from these modules. This approach effectively filters out noisy or static features that would otherwise distort cellular trajectory inference.
keywords: Two‑step process: dynamic module identification, trajectory‑based ranking
Local variation captures dynamic features, not global variance
Filters static/noisy features prior to trajectory construction
Outperforms traditional methods overwhelmed by noise

content: In systematic benchmarks on simulated and real single‑cell datasets, DELVE consistently outperforms 11 existing feature selection methods across multiple metrics: it achieves higher precision@k for differentially expressed genes, higher k‑NN classification accuracy for cell types, and higher pseudotime correlation with ground truth cell progression. These advantages hold across linear, bifurcating, and tree‑structured trajectories.
keywords: Compared against 11 other methods (supervised, similarity‑based, variance‑based)
Metrics: precision@k, classification accuracy, pseudotime correlation
Tested on linear, bifurcating, and tree topologies

content: DELVE exhibits strong robustness to common single‑cell noise sources, including low signal‑to‑noise ratio, reduced library size, and increased dropout. In simulated experiments, DELVE shows the smallest performance degradation as noise increases, whereas similarity‑based methods like Laplacian Score and MCFS suffer substantial declines. This resilience is crucial for analyzing noisy experimental data.
keywords: Robust to low SNR, small library size, high dropout
Minimal performance drop under increasing noise
Contrasts with Laplacian Score, MCFS which degrade sharply

content: PHATE visualization using all features from the RPE cell‑cycle dataset shows a diffuse embedding with poor separation of cell cycle phases (G0/G1, S, G2, M). This baseline highlights the need for feature selection to resolve continuous biological trajectories.

PHATE visualization using DELVE‑selected features (top 30) on the RPE dataset reveals a smooth, continuous trajectory that tightly clusters cells by cell cycle phase. DELVE achieves high classification accuracy (0.96), NMI (0.60), and pseudotime correlation (0.66), demonstrating its ability to recover biologically meaningful progression from noisy imaging data.
keywords: Smooth trajectory with tight phase clusters
Quantitative metrics: accuracy 0.96, NMI 0.60, pseudotime correlation 0.66
